mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes, with Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a 60-day forward return mean below zero over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of cases when INDPRO declines during high inflation periods, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL > 300.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that stabilizes equity discount factors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL annualized growth by > 1.5%
failure_condition: The spread signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals economic contraction that triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 70% of instances following sub-1% GDP growth, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear markets.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable retail sales indicates efficient supply chain utilization that expands corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day forward returns when INDPRO rises and RSAFS is flat, with a mean return below -0.5% over a 4-year sample.
